title: Notopleura uliginosa
taxonomy: Notopleura uliginosa belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Notopleura is a part of the Rubiaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Notopleura uliginosa is further classified under the order Gentianales and class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Notopleura uliginosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Notopleura uliginosa is primarily used in traditional medicine in South America, particularly in Brazil and Peru. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Notopleura uliginosa is used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Its extracts are also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and as an anti-parasitic agent. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Notopleura uliginosa.
